abstract: Neville Li from Spotify will present: Scio: Scala on the Google Cloud Scio (http://github.com/spotify/scio) is a Scala API for Google Cloud Dataflow inspired by Spark and Scalding. It unleashes the power of Google Cloud big data products with a concise and elegant Scala DSL. We’ll discuss the unified batch and streaming model, core API design, and additional features such as type safe BigQuery, HDFS, and REPL. There’ll also be a live coding demo. Speaker: Neville has been working at Spotify for 5 years now and is a member of the music recommendation team. He also leads the effort of adopting various Scala frameworks and functional programming techniques for data processing. Lightbend's Play team will present Play 2.5: Play 2.5: More Streams, More Speed, Less State Come see what's new in Play 2.5! Play is an asynchronous, non-blocking web framework and 2.5 brings a whole raft of goodies with it. Some major points: • Akka Streams integration, like Iteratees but much easier to understand • Additions to Java HTTP APIs • Performance improvement of up to 20% • Internal upgrading to Netty 4.0 and AsyncHttpClient 2.0 • Method and classes that depend on global state singleton object have been deprecated Documentation and Template Scrubbing Speakers: Will Sargent and Greg Methvin work at Lightbend on the Play team. Please check out Will's in-progress book Play in Practice (http://shop.oreilly.com/product/0636920035718.do) and download the sample chapters. Due to the packed program, we shift everything up 30 minutes: doors open at 6, and talks begin at 6:30pm sharp. This event will be full so please manage your RSVP responsibly. We're using a powerful set of single-node small data systems to make sure RSVPs are fairly utilized
event_title: Scala on the Google Cloud and Play 2.5
title: Scala on the Google Cloud and Play 2.5
